items.title: FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised
items.summary:
The large-scale credential theft campaign hit roughly half of the internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls and VPNs.
The post FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA: Splunk Enterprise flaw actively exploited, patch by Sunday
items.summary: CISA has urged U.S. federal agencies to secure their systems by Sunday against a critical Splunk Enterprise vulnerability that is being exploited in attacks. [...]

items.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:
The hackers exfiltrated data from Salesforce instances of Klue customers, such as Huntress and Recorded Future.
The post Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
items.summary:
Salesforce has revealed that it disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration within its platform in response to a security incident impacting the competitive intelligence company on June 11, 2026.

To that end, organizations will be unable to connect to Salesforce via the app until further notice, the American cloud-based software company noted in an alert published this week.

items.title: NY man charged after harassing college student with AI-generated nudes
items.summary: A New York man faces cyberstalking charges after allegedly sharing AI-generated nude images and fabricated racist messages using fake social media profiles to harass a Georgia college student. [...]

items.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn’t
items.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

items.title: Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC
items.summary:
WideField will accelerate Agentic SOC capabilities by expanding the lens on threat investigation to include identity, credentials, sessions, and blast radius.
The post Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak
items.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) urged Fortinet customers to secure their devices after nearly 74,000 firewall and VPN credentials were exposed in a data leak dubbed "FortiBleed." [...]

items.title: 15,000 WordPress Websites Cleaned Up in SocGholish Botnet Takedown
items.summary:
Law enforcement and private partners took down 106 SocGholish C&C servers and domains as part of Operation Endgame.
The post 15,000 WordPress Websites Cleaned Up in SocGholish Botnet Takedown  appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple Patches Beats Studio Buds Flaw Letting Nearby Attackers Spy via Microphone
items.summary:
Apple has updated its Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds to patch a high-severity vulnerability that could be exploited by nearby hackers to eavesdrop on users.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-20701 (CVSS score: 8.8), refers to a case of incorrect authorization impacting the Airoha Bluetooth audio SDK that makes it possible to pair a Bluetooth audio device without user consent.

items.title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
items.summary: Five months after returning to OpenAI, Barret Zoph - the company's head of enterprise AI sales - has departed, The Verge has learned. Zoph returned to OpenAI in mid-January after a stint as co-founder and CTO of Thinking Machines Lab, the competing AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati. items.title: Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure
items.summary:
CISA has given federal agencies only three days to patch CVE-2026-20253, which can be exploited for unauthenticated remote code execution.
The post Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
items.summary: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

items.title: Datasette Apps: Host custom HTML applications inside Datasette
items.summary:
Today we launched a new plugin for Datasette, datasette-apps, with this launch announcement post on the Datasette project blog. That post has the what, but I'm going to expand on that a little bit here to provide the why.
The TL;DR
Datasette Apps are self-contained HTML+JavaScript applications that run in a tightly constrained <iframe> sandbox hosted on your Datasette application. They can use JavaScript to run read-only SQL queries against data in Datasette, and can run write queries too if you configure them with some stored queries.

items.title: Gentlemen ransomware uses multiple EDR killers to disable defenses
items.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers to help affiliates evade detection in attacks. [...]

items.title: Announcing Amazon EC2 G7 instances accelerated by NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs
items.summary: Announcing the general availability of Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) G7 instances, delivering high performance GPU acceleration for AI inference, graphics, and data analytics workloads.

items.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
items.summary: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.
